slice.name: account-registration-request
slice.scope: Public account request, Team Admin review queue and registration-without-role-assignment controls

user_goal: Let users request a ResearchOps account without any role being applied, while giving a Team Admin enough information to review the request.
safe_reasoning_summary: Treat the journey as an access request, not as a role-selection transaction.
Use activity-based copy so the user says what they need to use ResearchOps for rather than choosing a system role.
Keep the public submit route unauthenticated and the review route protected by role.assign.
Store requested role key and label as review metadata only.
Add a check-answers step before sending the request.
Add tests proving the registration route does not write to auth_role_assignments.
key_decisions: Remove the registration page notification banner and move directly relevant before-you-start content into body copy.
Change the main role-like question to 'What do you need to use ResearchOps for?'.
Add a check-answers section before sending the request.
Return HTTP 200 and created:false when a duplicate pending request already exists.
Keep Team Admin review read-only in this slice and route role assignment through the existing role-assignment page.
Document the slice in docs/product and record dual-format agent trace artefacts.
validation_added: Route-state assertions for worker routing, migration shape, review-only language, check-answers behaviour and separation from role assignment.
Runtime tests using a fake D1 store for valid registration, duplicate pending request and invalid email handling.
Runtime assertion that registration request handling does not prepare SQL touching auth_role_assignments.
boundaries: No approval, rejection, mark-as-reviewed or more-information-needed workflow yet.
No rate limiting, domain allow-listing or public-route abuse control yet.
No requester or team-admin email notifications yet.
Visual walkthrough report regeneration remains outstanding.
residual_risks: The team admin review page is read-only and does not close out the request queue after a role is assigned.
The public submit route needs abuse protection before release.
Team or service is still free text and may create inconsistent operational queues.
The visual walkthrough registry should be expanded before release gating.
